children.label: A

children.heading: Interagency Group on Freely Associated States
children.label: d Interagency Group on Freely Associated States

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The President, in consultation with the Secretary of State, the Secretary of the Interior, and the Secretary of Defense, shall establish an Interagency Group on Freely Associated States (referred to in this subsection as the “Interagency Group”).

children.label: A
children.text: to coordinate development and implementation of executive branch policies, programs, services, and other activities in or relating to the Freely Associated States; and

children.label: i
children.text: the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact;

children.label: ii
children.text: the 2023 Amended U.S.-RMI Compact; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the 2023 U.S.-Palau Compact Review Agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of State, who shall serve as co-chair of the Interagency Group;

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior, who shall serve as co-chair of the Interagency Group;

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary of Defense;

children.label: D
children.text: the Secretary of the Treasury;

children.label: i
children.text: sections 131 and 132 of the 2003 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact and subsections (a) and (b) of section 221 and section 261 of the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact;

children.label: ii
children.text: sections 131 and 132 of the 2003 Amended U.S.-RMI Compact and subsections (a) and (b) of section 221 and section 261 of the 2023 Amended U.S.-RMI Compact;

children.label: iii
children.text: sections 131 and 132 and subsections (a) and (b) of section 221 of the U.S.-Palau Compact;

children.label: iv
children.text: Article 6 of the 2023 U.S.-Palau Compact Review Agreement;

children.label: v
children.text: any applicable subsidiary agreement; and

children.label: vi
children.text: ; andsection 1988 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: the head of any other Federal agency, department, or instrumentality that the Secretary of State or the Secretary of the Interior may designate.

children.heading: Duties of Secretary of State and Secretary of the Interior
children.label: 4 Duties of Secretary of State and Secretary of the Interior
A
children.text: co-lead and preside at a meeting of the Interagency Group not less frequently than annually;

children.label: B
children.text: determine, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, the agenda for meetings of the Interagency Group; and

children.label: C
children.text: facilitate and coordinate the work of the Interagency Group.

children.label: A
children.text: provide advice on the establishment or implementation of policies relating to the Freely Associated States to the President, acting through the Office of Intergovernmental Affairs, in the form of a written report not less frequently than annually;

children.label: B
children.text: obtain information and advice relating to the Freely Associated States from the Presidents, other elected officials, and members of civil society of the Freely Associated States, including through the members of the Interagency Group (including senior official designees of the members) meeting not less frequently than annually with any Presidents of the Freely Associated States who elect to participate;

children.label: C
children.text: at the request of the head of any Federal agency (or a senior official designee of the head of a Federal agency) who is a member of the Interagency Group, promptly review and provide advice on a policy or policy implementation action affecting 1 or more of the Freely Associated States proposed by the Federal agency, department, or instrumentality; and

children.label: D
children.text: facilitate coordination of relevant policies, programs, initiatives, and activities involving 1 or more of the Freely Associated States, including ensuring coherence and avoiding duplication between programs, initiatives, and activities conducted pursuant to a Compact with a Freely Associated State and non-Compact programs, initiatives, and activities.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 6 Reports
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , and each year thereafter in which a Compact of Free Association with a Freely Associated State is in effect, the President shall submit to the majority leader and minority leader of the Senate, the Speaker and minority leader of the House of Representatives, and the appropriate committees of Congress a report that describes the activities and recommendations of the Interagency Group during the applicable year.March 9, 2024

children.heading: Federal agency coordination
children.label: e Federal agency coordination
children.text: The head of any Federal agency providing programs and services to the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, or the Republic of Palau shall coordinate with the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of State regarding the provision of the programs and services.

the foreign loans or debt of the Government of the Federated States of Micronesia, the Government of the Republic of the Marshall Islands, or the Government of the Republic of Palau shall not constitute an obligation of the United States; and

the full faith and credit of the United States Government shall not be pledged for the payment and performance of any foreign loan or debt referred to in paragraph (1) without specific further authorization.

children.heading: Compact compilation
children.label: g Compact compilation
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of the Interior shall submit a report to the appropriate committees of Congress that includes a compilation of the Compact of Free Association with the Federated State of Micronesia, the Compact of Free Association with the Republic of Palau, and the Compact of Free Association with Republic of the Marshall Islands.March 9, 2024
